problems.name: Uncertain Diagnosis
problems.description: Intermittent or unclear symptoms make definitive diagnosis difficult or impossible without extended observation
impacts: Multiple repair attempts possible if root cause misidentified
Customer frustration with inconclusive initial report
typicalResponses: Request extended test drive to reproduce the problem
Recommend monitoring with a scheduled follow-up
Propose phased diagnostic approach starting with most likely cause
Suggest leaving vehicle for a full day for observation

problems.name: Parts Unavailable
problems.description: Required parts are not in stock locally and must be ordered, causing repair delays
impacts: Repair timeline extends by days or weeks
Customer vehicle unavailable longer than expected
Potential need to consider aftermarket alternatives
typicalResponses: Check alternative local suppliers for faster availability
Offer aftermarket part as temporary solution with customer consent
Provide loaner vehicle if available (subject to owner approval)
Give accurate timeline estimate and regular updates

problems.name: Customer Cost Objection
problems.description: Customer disagrees with the repair cost and pushes back on the estimate
impacts: Revenue loss risk if customer walks away
Risk of deferred critical repairs creating liability
typicalResponses: Explain repair necessity with specific technical justification
Offer phased repair plan prioritizing most critical items
Discuss payment plan options (subject to owner approval)
Provide detailed breakdown to show cost transparency

problems.name: Customer Refuses Safety-Critical Repair
problems.description: Customer declines a repair that is critical for vehicle safety
impacts: Legal and liability risk for the shop
Ethical obligation to clearly communicate risk
Potential refusal to return vehicle without disclaimer
typicalResponses: Document the refusal in writing with customer signature
Clearly explain safety risks in non-technical language
Escalate to owner for final decision on liability acceptance
Offer to perform only non-critical repairs from the estimate

problems.name: Additional Problems Found During Repair
problems.description: Mechanic discovers additional problems not visible in the original diagnostic
impacts: Customer may feel surprised or misled
Additional approval cycle required before proceeding
typicalResponses: Stop work and contact customer immediately
Explain what was found and why it was not visible initially
Provide updated estimate before proceeding
Prioritize additional work by urgency
